meta.productName: PE Rule-Out SMART on FHIR Integration

objective: Reduce unnecessary CT-PA ordering by giving ED clinicians a SMART on FHIR-integrated clinical decision support tool that surfaces PE rule-out risk assessments directly within the clinician's EHR workflow.
userPersonas: Emergency Department Physician – primary decision-maker, needs fast actionable summaries during high-volume shifts.
(Future) Clinical Informaticist – configures thresholds and rule sets, needs editable documentation and audit trails.
successMetrics: ≥ 80% of ED physicians rate the tool as helpful in pilot survey
Reduction in CT-PA orders for PERC-negative patients, Wells PE unlikely patients, and YEARS rule out patients
SMART launch succeeds in Epic sandbox 100% of the time
Zero PHI stored beyond the session lifetime
keyFeatures: SMART on FHIR OAuth 2.0 launch from Epic EHR context.
PERC, Wells, and YEARS score calculation from FHIR data and/or clinical input
Clinical snapshot: vitals, D-dimer, prior imaging, and hemodynamics.
Editable clinical thresholds with audit trail.
userStories: As an ED physician, I want to see a PE risk score when I open a patient chart, so that I can quickly decide whether to order a CT-PA.
As a clinician, I want to see which labs and vitals drove the score, so that I can trust and explain the recommendation.
As a clinical informaticist, I want to edit risk thresholds in-app, so that I can tune the tool without a code deployment.
As an ED physician reviewing multiple patients, I want a pre-chart dashboard, so that I can triage by risk before entering each chart.
uxAndDesign: Don't overuse alert colors to avoid alert fatigue
Avoid interruptive popups whenever possible to avoid alert fatigue
Mobile-responsive layout in case of tablet use at the bedside.
(outstanding) - what are launch options for FHIR app? Side panel view for modals, and popup for piechart? Need to check if any restrictions from health system.
(outstanding) - override reasons within CDS cards - should we use those? What are override reasons if so? Does that help/hurt physicians, patients, hospital?
inScope: SMART on FHIR OAuth launch and token refresh.
outOfScope: PHI storage or persistence beyond in-memory session.
technicalRequirements: Frontend: React + TypeScript, Vite build, Tailwind / inline styles.
Backend: Python FastAPI, served over HTTPS in production.
FHIR R4 compliance for all resource queries.
SMART on FHIR v2 (authorization_code with PKCE).
Model: scikit-learn pipeline serialized with joblib.
All secrets via environment variables, never committed.
dependencies: Epic FHIR sandbox credentials and app registration.
FHIR R4 sandbox patient data for testing.
Institutional compliance review before any patient data use.
Pilot health system IT buy in and clarity on where CDS cards will be placed and options for SMART on FHIR app launch
timeline: Week 1–2: SMART launch + OAuth flow working in sandbox.
Week 3–4: FHIR data mapping and score calculations.
Week 5–6: Clinical snapshot UI and decision card.
Week 8: Editable docs, polish, internal pilot.
openQuestions: What are our options for where the app can launch in the EPIC UI?
How will we get pilot data to evaluate results?
How will we get retroactive data to analyze before the pilot?
supportingDocuments: Clinical Thresholds doc (see Docs > Clinical Thresholds)

decisionLog.decision: Use SMART on FHIR with CDS cards over BPAs
decisionLog.rationale: Reduces a dependency on health system for BPA setup
